title: Aliens vs Ancient Civilizations vs Giants & Atlantis & such

enrichment.summary: Kyle shares his personal theories on Atlantis, giants, and alien involvement in ancient history. He argues Atlantis was a real civilization located in what is now the shallow Caribbean near Andros Island, that giants evolved naturally through island gigantism in Siberia, and that while aliens exist and have been observed, they operate under a prime directive and did not significantly intervene in human development. He weaves together Plato, Graham Hancock, Pliny, Herodotus, genetics, and Native American flood legends to support a Darwinian but open-minded view of deep prehistory.
enrichment.speaker: kyle

stances.position: Literally real; located near Andros Island in the Caribbean, sunk ~11,600 years ago at the Younger Dryas event, matching Plato's dimensions exactly
Aliens exist and were present as observers, not interveners; prime directive explains why they didn't shape civilization
Naturally evolved through Darwinian island/environment gigantism in Siberia; related to Denisovans; confirmed by Pliny and Native American legends
Respects it as a valid scientific approach; agrees on lost ancient civilization but does not necessarily share his alien skepticism
The ancient world was far more globally connected than mainstream history acknowledges, citing Irish Book of Invasions and Egyptian circumnavigation of Africa
Cro-Magnons had bigger brains and bodies than modern humans, suggesting we have cognitively and physically devolved since the ice ages
Possibly an alien observer species, though skeptical due to bear misidentification in the Pacific Northwest
Ancient flood myths and legends should be taken as distorted but real historical accounts, not pure fiction

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: It's been forever since I've done a video talking about philosophical topics like aliens, giants, and past histories.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I was studying some histories before and had some insights.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I'm kind of stoned right now.

claims.subject: aliens
claims.claim: The question is at what point along the spectrum does truth lie in pertinence to how real are aliens and how much of an interaction with us have they had?

claims.subject: giants
claims.claim: How alien are giants from the past and how native to the earth, like in a scientific way of looking at the world, are they?

claims.subject: giants and aliens
claims.claim: Believing in giants and aliens isn't necessarily on the same category, but a lot of people like to talk about those two things together.

claims.subject: Graham Hancock
claims.claim: Graham Hancock believes in a past world civilization but looks at it from a scientific way and isn't into aliens at all.

claims.subject: Graham Hancock
claims.claim: Graham Hancock doesn't want to talk about Roswell, Bob Lazar, or the Face on Mars.

claims.subject: mainstream scientists
claims.claim: In the future, more mainstream scientists will believe in giants being real, Atlantis, and similar ideas.

claims.subject: Atlantis
claims.claim: The legend of Atlantis comes from Plato, who is the only actual source for Atlantis.

claims.subject: Plato
claims.claim: Plato said that Atlantis sunk 9,000 years before Solon, who was around 6,000 BC, making it 11,600 years ago.

claims.subject: Younger Dryas
claims.claim: Atlantis sunk right at the beginning of the Younger Dryas, when the ice age ended suddenly, 85% of animals bigger than a dog died, and sea levels rose.

claims.subject: Atlantis
claims.claim: Atlantis was an island beyond the Pillars of Hercules, having a war with Athens, and was at least as technologically advanced and worldly as ancient civilizations.

claims.subject: Atlantis
claims.claim: Atlantis was larger than the ancient world until now because they were seafaring between the Americas and Europe.

claims.subject: Christopher Columbus
claims.claim: If you do the Christopher Columbus thing and sail beyond the Pillars of Hercules, you reach the island next to where Atlantis is in about three weeks.

claims.subject: Andros
claims.claim: The island of Andros in the Caribbean is next to where Atlantis is, and if you lower the water by 60 feet, the island would emerge.

claims.subject: Plato
claims.claim: Plato described Atlantis as 550 by 350 kilometers, which matches the size of the underwater island near Andros.

claims.subject: Atlantis
claims.claim: There is a coincidence between Plato's description of Atlantis and the underwater island near Andros in terms of size and location.

claims.subject: Plato
claims.claim: Plato described the city of Atlantis with inner circles, walls, and canals, which match features found near Andros.

claims.subject: Atlantis
claims.claim: The city of Atlantis was about the size of greater Rome or Denver.

claims.subject: Atlantis
claims.claim: Plato's description of Atlantis includes 1500 kilometers of canals, which could fit on the island near Andros.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I take Plato's account of Atlantis literally as a fundamentalist historian.

claims.subject: Atlantis
claims.claim: If you believe Atlantis existed 11,600 years ago, it implies a world civilization even grander than ancient Rome at its height.

claims.subject: Atlantis
claims.claim: Atlantis would have been an ideal place for a society because it was an insulated island like England or Japan.

claims.subject: ice age predators
claims.claim: In the ice age, there were gigantic bears, mountain lions, saber-toothed tigers, and sloths that would hunt humans.

claims.subject: Cahokia
claims.claim: Cahokia mound near St. Louis was built about a thousand years ago, but the river nearby looks ancient.

claims.subject: giants
claims.claim: Giants could have evolved in Siberia during the ice ages to hunt mastodons and fight off predators.

claims.subject: giants
claims.claim: Giants may have struggled when they moved south and had to live among regular-sized humans, leading to myths about cannibalism.

claims.subject: mastodons
claims.claim: Mastodons and saber-toothed tigers may have gone extinct due to overhunting by starving giants.

claims.subject: giants
claims.claim: I believe in giants because of stories from Native Americans and historical accounts.

claims.subject: Smithsonian Institute
claims.claim: Thousands of giant bones were stockpiled in the Smithsonian Institute but were given back to Native American tribes about 10 years ago.

claims.subject: Denisovans
claims.claim: A Denisovan tooth found in Siberia suggests the person was about 10 feet tall.

claims.subject: Gigantopithecus
claims.claim: Gigantopithecus was a giant primate ancestor of the orangutan, showing that primates can get giant.

claims.subject: Neanderthals
claims.claim: Neanderthals went extinct about 35,000 to 40,000 years ago because they didn't organize as well as Homo sapiens.

claims.subject: Pliny
claims.claim: Pliny said that in Roman times, it was common knowledge that children were shorter than their parents, implying the giant genome was still present.

claims.subject: Herodotus
claims.claim: Herodotus tells a story of King Gyges finding a giant's tomb in Lydia.

claims.subject: ancient Rome
claims.claim: In ancient Rome, there was a 12-foot-tall man and wife who led an army in the Middle East.

claims.subject: Irish Book of Invasions
claims.claim: The Irish Book of Invasions describes people from Scythia invading Ireland in 30 boats around 2,500 BC.

claims.subject: ancient Egypt
claims.claim: Ancient Egyptians circumnavigated Africa by boat, proving advanced seafaring in ancient times.

claims.subject: Cro-Magnon
claims.claim: Cro-Magnon humans, our ancestors, were taller and had bigger heads than us, implying they were smarter.

claims.subject: human evolution
claims.claim: Humanity may have devolved in brain size and intelligence since Cro-Magnon times.

claims.subject: Bigfoot
claims.claim: Bigfoot might be an intelligent alien observing us, according to Bashar.

claims.subject: Amazon rainforest
claims.claim: Francisco de Orellana wrote about encountering gigantic, muscular, topless women in the Amazon rainforest.

claims.subject: Native American legends
claims.claim: Native American flood stories describe events that match the end of the ice age 12,000 years ago.

claims.subject: Mandan tribe
claims.claim: The Mandan tribe has a legend of a man arriving from Atlantis after it was flooded.

claims.subject: aliens
claims.claim: Aliens may have observed humans throughout history but didn't interfere, explaining sightings in ancient texts.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I think the proper way to look at alien influence is through mainstream Darwinian evolution, with aliens as observers.
